mnemonics: VACUUM PUMPS SUCK IN EVERY BIT: Visualize a vacuum pump (VACUUM) sucking in primitive data types. PUMPS = byte(8), short(16), int(32), long(64). SUCK = float(32), char(16). IN = int, double(64). EVERY = boolean. BIT = all are bits in memory.
keyPoints: Primitive types are stored by value, not by reference
Default values exist for primitive types when declared as instance variables (e.g., int defaults to 0)
Wrapper classes (Integer, Double, etc.) provide object representations of primitive types
commonMistakes: Using == to compare objects instead of .equals() method
Forgetting that char uses single quotes ('') while String uses double quotes (")
Attempting to use a primitive type where an object is required (e.g., in Collections)
